message:
feat(abi): box NativeFile mutex for stable lock pointer and expand test coverage

Core change: NativeFileState now wraps its ReentrantMutex in a Box to
provide a heap-stable address for the glibc _IO_FILE._lock pointer:

- Box<ReentrantMutex<...>> ensures the lock address survives moves of
  NativeFileState, matching glibc's assumption that _lock is stable
- Add lock_ptr() method returning a *mut c_void for the boxed mutex
- Add sync_lock_ptr() to NativeFile that writes the stable address into
  _io_file._lock during construction
- NativeFile::new() now calls sync_lock_ptr() automatically
- Format cleanup: parking_lot import ordering, layout test assertion

Test expansion (+180 lines):
- Extend io_internal_native_file_test.rs with additional edge case coverage
  for the native file ABI surface

Config updates:
- Update MCP agent-mail endpoint from /api/ to /mcp/ in cursor.mcp.json
  and gemini.mcp.json (reflects server route migration)
- Rotate bearer tokens for local MCP agent-mail sessions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

2) Rank by runtime impact and replacement complexity.

- Decommission plan is dependency-aware and implementation-ready.

 {"id":"bd-9chy","title":"stdio: replace Host*Fn delegation with native NativeFile path (Option B)","description":"## Background

The 2026-04-08 reality-check audit (see STDIO_NATIVE_REPLACEMENT_PLAN.md v4 in repo root) identified that the README headline claim "100.0% native coverage / 0 GlibcCallThrough" is technically true under the support matrixs current labelling rule but materially false at runtime: when prefer_host_stdio_streams() returns true (the default in LD_PRELOAD mode), fopen() and fdopen() delegate to host libc and return host glibc FILE* pointers via 21 host_*_fn function-pointer slots in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs L30-71. From that moment on every fread/fwrite/fclose/fseek on that stream is forced to delegate too, because no FrankenLibC stream registry entry exists for an alien pointer.

This epic tracks the work to make the native Rust stdio kernel in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/ (4149 LOC, real and tested in isolation via io_internal_*_use_native_stdio_paths tests at stdio_abi_test.rs L2443-2650) the *only* runtime path, so that the headline number becomes literally true with a body-inspecting classifier and a witness-chain audit.

## Why this matters

The projects own Design Invariants explicitly state: "Documentation and machine artifacts are expected to agree — drift is treated as a bug." The current state violates this invariant. Closing the gap is the highest-leverage single piece of honesty work the project can do: it converts a misleading marketing claim into a mechanically verifiable scalar.

## Scope (per plan v4)

In scope:
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs — eliminate Host*Fn delegation
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs — extend NativeFile to _IO_FILE_plus layout
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs L313/324/348/358 — replace libc::fread/fwrite in 4 XDR adapters
- crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/glibc_internal_abi.rs L5197/5267 — replace libc::fwrite in fputgrent_r/fgetspent_r
- All 9 FILE-acquisition channels: fopen, fdopen, freopen, popen, tmpfile, fmemopen, open_memstream, open_wmemstream, fopencookie
- _IO_2_1_stdin_/stdout_/stderr_ alias exports in version_scripts/libc.map
- Foreign-pointer adoption (channel 12: dlopend libraries that opened files before LD_PRELOAD took effect)
- _IO_jump_t vtable for indirect-dispatch-callers
- Per-FILE recursive lock + flockfile/funlockfile family
- Wide-char orientation + mbstate_t per stream
- Append-mode atomicity, EINTR loops, signal-handler write policy
- Body-inspecting support_matrix classifier
- Un-skip sqlite3/redis-cli/nginx in ld_preload_smoke
- Continuous criterion bench gate for the <20ns/<200ns membrane budgets
- Healing oracle stdio scenarios
- Witness-chain audit and rendered README counts
- Offline synthesis pipeline for printf/scanf with SMT proof obligations + Atiyah-Bott DAG compression
- POMDP repair as the primary hardened-mode dispatcher for stdio
- Closure contract stdio block
- Cross-subsystem ripple to setjmp/signal/pthread/process/startup/fortify/io_internal/wchar

Out of scope:
- scandir_r host calls in glibc_internal_abi.rs L5518-5569 (separate dirent epic)
- Locale-aware printf beyond LC_NUMERIC decimal-point handling
- C++ iostreams
- Multi-arch validation (Linux x86_64 only)

## Done criteria

See STDIO_NATIVE_REPLACEMENT_PLAN.md §9 (v4) for the complete checklist. Headline:

- rg -n host_.*_fn|libc::f.* crates/frankenlibc-abi/ returns zero matches outside wchar parameter signatures
- support_matrix.json reports WrapsHostLibc == 0 from a body-inspecting classifier
- LD_PRELOAD smoke battery is 64/0/0 (sqlite3 + redis-cli + nginx green in strict and hardened)
- cargo bench --bench stdio_native_fastpath passes <20ns strict / <200ns hardened budgets
- Healing oracle stdio cases all green in hardened mode
- The README "100.0% native coverage" number is rendered from reality_report.v1.json by scripts/render_readme_counts.py, never hand-typed
- closure_contract.v1.json carries the stdio block from plan §18 and check_closure_contract.sh is green
- tests/runtime_math/golden/stdio_decisions.v1.json and io_jump_dispatch.v1.json exist and are stable

## Plan-doc reference

Full design rationale, formal model (lattice + Galois), proof obligations, math-stack mapping, POMDP design, synthesis pipeline architecture, witness chain, rollback story, and risk register live in STDIO_NATIVE_REPLACEMENT_PLAN.md v4 in the repo root. Phase 5 refinement of these beads should re-read that doc; the doc is the durable spec. Beads should never need to repeat the full design rationale, only the per-task-actionable subset.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:25:41.509856313Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:25:41.509856313Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["option-b","reality-check","stdio"]}
-{"id":"bd-9chy.1","title":"stdio: extend NativeFile to _IO_FILE_plus layout with vtable + recursive lock + mbstate","description":"## Background

The audit confirms NativeFile already exists at crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs L66-116 (288 bytes, with a static assertion at L123 that size >= 216 bytes hinting at glibc _IO_FILE compat). v4 plan §3 commits to making it a true _IO_FILE_plus layout: first 216 bytes match glibc 2.34 _IO_FILE byte-for-byte, then a vtable pointer slot, then FrankenLibC-only state.

## What to do

1. Define a private repr(C) struct _IO_FILE_Layout in io_internal_abi.rs whose field offsets exactly match glibc 2.34 _IO_FILE: _flags, _IO_read_ptr, _IO_read_end, _IO_read_base, _IO_write_base, _IO_write_ptr, _IO_write_end, _IO_buf_base, _IO_buf_end, _IO_save_base, _IO_backup_base, _IO_save_end, _markers, _chain, _fileno, _flags2, _old_offset, _cur_column, _vtable_offset, _shortbuf, _lock, _offset, _codecvt, _wide_data, _freeres_list, _freeres_buf, _pad5, _mode, _unused2.
2. Use memoffset::offset_of! macros to static_assert! every field offset against the glibc 2.34 numbers (collect numbers from /usr/include/bits/types/struct_FILE.h on a glibc 2.34 host; pin them as constants in the test file).
3. Make NativeFile = repr(C) struct { _io_file: _IO_FILE_Layout, vtable: *mut _IO_jump_t, _frankenlibc_state: NativeFileState } where NativeFileState contains the parking_lot::ReentrantMutex<NativeFileLocked> with: buffer cursor (head/tail/cap/data ptr), eof/error flags, ungetc Option<u8>, generation u64, healing budget u32, mbstate_t, orientation AtomicI8 (-1 byte / 0 undecided / +1 wide), open flags (O_APPEND/O_NONBLOCK/O_SYNC/O_DSYNC), 16-byte fingerprint, runtime_math hooks slot.
4. static_assert! that size_of::<NativeFile>() <= 4096 (keeps slab small-class viable).
5. CI matrix: build and run the offset assertions against glibc 2.31, 2.34, 2.38 host headers; document any divergences.

## Why _IO_FILE_plus and not just _IO_FILE

Many glibc-built binaries call vfprintf indirectly through ((struct _IO_FILE_plus *)fp)->vtable->__xsputn(...) when compiled with -fno-plt. If our vtable pointer is null or points at glibcs vtable while the rest of the struct is FrankenLibC layout, the program crashes. Plan §3 + §10 detail.

## Done

- _IO_FILE_Layout is defined and field offsets match glibc 2.34
- NativeFile is the _IO_FILE_plus form
- All offset asserts pass on the CI matrix
- Existing NativeFile usage in io_internal_abi.rs and stdio_abi.rs compiles unchanged (the additions are appended, no removals)
- A unit test reads NativeFile fields through a *mut libc::FILE cast and verifies they match","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:26:07.221326833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:21:08.835548886Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.1","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:26:07.221326833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-9chy.1","title":"stdio: extend NativeFile to _IO_FILE_plus layout with vtable + recursive lock + mbstate","description":"## Background

The audit confirms NativeFile already exists at crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs L66-116 (288 bytes, with a static assertion at L123 that size >= 216 bytes hinting at glibc _IO_FILE compat). v4 plan §3 commits to making it a true _IO_FILE_plus layout: first 216 bytes match glibc 2.34 _IO_FILE byte-for-byte, then a vtable pointer slot, then FrankenLibC-only state.

## What to do

1. Define a private repr(C) struct _IO_FILE_Layout in io_internal_abi.rs whose field offsets exactly match glibc 2.34 _IO_FILE: _flags, _IO_read_ptr, _IO_read_end, _IO_read_base, _IO_write_base, _IO_write_ptr, _IO_write_end, _IO_buf_base, _IO_buf_end, _IO_save_base, _IO_backup_base, _IO_save_end, _markers, _chain, _fileno, _flags2, _old_offset, _cur_column, _vtable_offset, _shortbuf, _lock, _offset, _codecvt, _wide_data, _freeres_list, _freeres_buf, _pad5, _mode, _unused2.
2. Use memoffset::offset_of! macros to static_assert! every field offset against the glibc 2.34 numbers (collect numbers from /usr/include/bits/types/struct_FILE.h on a glibc 2.34 host; pin them as constants in the test file).
3. Make NativeFile = repr(C) struct { _io_file: _IO_FILE_Layout, vtable: *mut _IO_jump_t, _frankenlibc_state: NativeFileState } where NativeFileState contains the parking_lot::ReentrantMutex<NativeFileLocked> with: buffer cursor (head/tail/cap/data ptr), eof/error flags, ungetc Option<u8>, generation u64, healing budget u32, mbstate_t, orientation AtomicI8 (-1 byte / 0 undecided / +1 wide), open flags (O_APPEND/O_NONBLOCK/O_SYNC/O_DSYNC), 16-byte fingerprint, runtime_math hooks slot.
4. static_assert! that size_of::<NativeFile>() <= 4096 (keeps slab small-class viable).
5. CI matrix: build and run the offset assertions against glibc 2.31, 2.34, 2.38 host headers; document any divergences.

## Why _IO_FILE_plus and not just _IO_FILE

Many glibc-built binaries call vfprintf indirectly through ((struct _IO_FILE_plus *)fp)->vtable->__xsputn(...) when compiled with -fno-plt. If our vtable pointer is null or points at glibcs vtable while the rest of the struct is FrankenLibC layout, the program crashes. Plan §3 + §10 detail.

## Done

- _IO_FILE_Layout is defined and field offsets match glibc 2.34
- NativeFile is the _IO_FILE_plus form
- All offset asserts pass on the CI matrix
- Existing NativeFile usage in io_internal_abi.rs and stdio_abi.rs compiles unchanged (the additions are appended, no removals)
- A unit test reads NativeFile fields through a *mut libc::FILE cast and verifies they match","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:26:07.221326833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:32:18.690890664Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:32:18.690676639Z","close_reason":"All done criteria met: _IO_FILE_Layout defined with glibc 2.34 offsets, NativeFile is _IO_FILE_plus form, compile-time asserts pass, 41 integration tests pass including FILE* cast validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.1","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:26:07.221326833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.10","title":"stdio: export _IO_2_1_stdin_/stdout_/stderr_ aliases bound to NativeFile storage","description":"## Background

Plan v4 §3 (final paragraph). Some statically-linked-against-libc binaries resolve _IO_2_1_stdin_, _IO_2_1_stdout_, _IO_2_1_stderr_ directly via dlsym or link-time resolution, bypassing the public stdin/stdout/stderr aliases. Without exporting these names from FrankenLibC, the binary sees two distinct streams (one native FrankenLibC, one host glibc) and the program misbehaves subtly: partial flushes, lost output, double-close.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (NativeFile layout)

## What to do

1. Add the three symbol names to crates/frankenlibc-abi/version_scripts/libc.map under the GLIBC_2.2.5 version tag.
2. In stdio_abi.rs (or io_internal_abi.rs), declare the underscored names as #[no_mangle] static aliases pointing to the *same* NativeFile storage as the public stdin/stdout/stderr globals. Do NOT allocate a second set of NativeFile objects — they must be the same object so a flush via stdin reaches the same buffer as a flush via _IO_2_1_stdin_.
3. Verify the alias by building the .so and inspecting nm: nm -D target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so | grep _IO_2_1 must show the three symbols at the same address as stdin/stdout/stderr.
4. Add an integration test: build a small C program that takes both stdin and _IO_2_1_stdin_, asserts they are equal, and runs it under LD_PRELOAD.

## Done

- libc.map exports the three aliases
- nm verification passes
- Integration test passes
- The aliases survive a release build with strip+lto","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:28:41.061649453Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:28:41.061649453Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","stdio","version-script"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.10","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:28:41.061649453Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.10","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:28:41.061649453Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.11","title":"stdio: delete all 20 Host*Fn type aliases, statics, accessors, and prefer_host_stdio_streams","description":"## Background

Once every FILE-acquisition channel (bd-9chy.5 through bd-9chy.10) creates NativeFile objects, no legitimate caller will ever pass an alien FILE* into stdio_abi.rs again. The "check registry first; delegate to host if alien" branches in every entrypoint become dead code, as do the 20 Host*Fn aliases, the 20 OnceLock<usize> statics, the 20 host_*_fn() accessors, and the prefer_host_stdio_streams() machinery.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.5, bd-9chy.6, bd-9chy.7, bd-9chy.8, bd-9chy.9, bd-9chy.10 (every constructor must already produce NativeFile)

## What to do

1. crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs:
   - Delete L30-50 (20 type aliases HostFopenFn..HostSetvbufFn).
   - Delete L52-71 (20 statics HOST_FOPEN_FN..HOST_SETVBUF_FN).
   - Delete L91-96 (prefer_host_stdio_streams function).
   - Delete L392-519 (20 host_*_fn accessor functions).
   - Delete the alien-FILE branch in every public entrypoint:
     - fclose L733-815 (delete the host-delegate branch)
     - fflush L821-898
     - fgetc L901-1015
     - fputc L1017-1113
     - fgets L1115-1290
     - fputs L1292-1435
     - fread L1437-1575
     - fwrite L1576-1730
     - fseek L1738-1864
     - ftell L1867-1901
     - feof L1932-1950
     - ferror L1952-1970
     - clearerr L1972-1989
     - ungetc L1991-2013
     - fileno L2015-2043
     - setvbuf L2045-2137
     - getdelim L4272-4357
     - getline L4363-4423
     - fprintf L2722-2810
     - vfprintf L3260-3704
   - Rename init_host_stdio_streams() to init_native_stdio_streams() and drop HOST_STDIO_BOOTSTRAPPED atomic.
2. crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/host_resolve.rs (or wherever resolve_host_symbol_cached lives): grep for any other stdio symbols being resolved (fopen, fread, etc.). Burn them down. Update the bead description with what you found.
3. After the deletions, cargo check + cargo clippy must be clean.

## Why this is its own bead instead of being folded into the constructors

The deletion *can only happen* after every constructor lands. Trying to delete one entrypoint at a time creates an inconsistent intermediate state where some streams are native and some are still alien — a regression nightmare. This bead is the atomic cutover.

## Done

- Zero matches for `host_.*_fn` in stdio_abi.rs
- Zero matches for `prefer_host_stdio_streams` in the entire crates/frankenlibc-abi/ tree
- All ABI tests still pass (the alien-pointer branch was never reachable from a legitimate caller after constructors landed)
- File line count drops by ~600 lines (rough estimate: 20 aliases + 20 statics + 20 accessors + 20 alien branches × ~10 lines each)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:05.277004270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:19:50.061886211Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cleanup","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.11","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:05.277004270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.11","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:05.277004270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.11","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:19:49.805634386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.11","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:05.277004270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.11","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.50","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:19:50.061093049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.11","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:05.277004270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.11","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:05.277004270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.11","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:05.277004270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.11","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:05.277004270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.12","title":"rpc_abi: replace 4 libc::fread/fwrite calls in XDR stdio adapters with native_fread/native_fwrite","description":"## Background

The audit found 4 libc::fread/fwrite calls in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs at L313, L324, L348, L358. These are XDR stdio adapters: stdio_gi32 reads a 4-byte big-endian int from an XDR FILE*; stdio_pi32 writes one; stdio_gbytes reads N bytes; stdio_pbytes writes N bytes. The FILE* is opaque to the XDR code and now (after bd-9chy.11 lands) will always be a *mut NativeFile.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.11 (the host_*_fn deletion bead — after that, native_fread/native_fwrite must be exposed as pub(crate) helpers from stdio_abi.rs because the public ABI fns no longer have host fallbacks to inline)

## What to do

1. Refactor stdio_abi.rs to expose pub(crate) fn native_fread(buf: *mut c_void, size: usize, n: usize, fp: *mut NativeFile) -> usize and the corresponding native_fwrite. The bodies are the existing kernel logic from the public fread/fwrite ABI fns; this is a method extraction.
2. crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/rpc_abi.rs:
   - L313: replace `libc::fread(buf, 1, 4, f.cast())` with `crate::stdio_abi::native_fread(buf, 1, 4, f.cast())`.
   - L324: replace `libc::fwrite(buf, 1, 4, f.cast())` with `crate::stdio_abi::native_fwrite(buf, 1, 4, f.cast())`.
   - L348: same pattern with (a, 1, n, f).
   - L358: same.
3. Verify the existing rpc tests (if any — search crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/) still pass.
4. After this lands, the body-inspecting classifier from bd-9chy.2 must reclassify rpc symbols from WrapsHostLibc back to Implemented.

## Done

- Zero libc::fread/fwrite in rpc_abi.rs
- The 4 XDR adapters use native_fread/native_fwrite
- support_matrix regenerates with rpc symbols as Implemented","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:18.889633593Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:18.889633593Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cleanup","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.12","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:18.889633593Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.12","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:29:18.889633593Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-9chy.48","title":"test: foreign-pointer adoption stress + fuzz + concurrent dlopen scenario","description":"## Background

bd-9chy.16 implements adopt_foreign_file. This is a high-risk feature: a single bug in fd extraction or registry indexing can cause double-close, lost writes, or memory corruption. The test surface must be paranoid.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.16

## Test list

Synthetic foreign pointers:
1. Construct a fake _IO_FILE struct in test memory with _fileno=N, pass to FrankenLibCs fread, verify adoption produces a NativeFile with fd=N.
2. The same foreign pointer passed twice must return the *same* NativeFile (registry deduplication).
3. After fclose via the foreign pointer, calling fread on the foreign pointer again must return EBADF (stale-pointer detection).

Real dlopen scenarios:
4. dlopen("libc.so.6"), dlsym fopen, open a file, pass FILE* to FrankenLibCs fread → adoption + correct read.
5. dlopen a third-party library that opens a file in its constructor (before LD_PRELOAD-ed binary main runs), then asks the binary to fread it → adoption.
6. Two threads concurrently call adopt_foreign_file on the same foreign pointer — only one NativeFile should be created (use a registry-level mutex during adoption, or compare-and-swap).

Fuzzing:
7. cargo fuzz target: random byte sequences cast to *mut FILE, passed to fread/fwrite/fseek. Must never crash; must always either succeed (after adoption) or return -1 with errno set. Target must run for 5 minutes in CI.

Generation safety:
8. Adopt foreign pointer A pointing at fd=7. Close fd=7 externally (raw close syscall via dlsym). Reopen fd=7 (it gets the same number). Call fread via the original foreign pointer A — must return EBADF (stale generation), NOT operate on the new fd.

Evidence:
9. Every adoption emits a FOREIGN_FILE_ADOPTED record per the schema in bd-9chy.4. Verify by reading target/conformance/stdio_evidence.jsonl after each test.
10. Stale-pointer reads emit FOREIGN_FILE_STALE.

## Done

- All 10 test categories in tests/integration/foreign_pointer_adoption_test.rs
- Fuzz target runs 5+ min in CI without crashes
- Evidence records verified for each scenario","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:40:42.170949883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:40:42.170949883Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foreign-adoption","stdio","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.48","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:40:42.170949883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.48","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:40:42.170949883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.49","title":"test: POMDP stdio dispatcher determinism + golden decision snapshot + belief-update unit tests","description":"## Background

bd-9chy.19 wires pomdp_repair as the primary hardened-mode stdio dispatcher. AGENTS.md "Design Invariants" requires hardened repairs to be deterministic. Determinism is non-trivial for a POMDP because the belief state depends on call history. This bead is the test surface that proves determinism + correctness.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.19

## Test list

Determinism:
1. Run the same hardened-mode stdio sequence twice with the same seed; assert byte-equal POMDP action sequences.
2. Run with two different seeds; assert action sequences may differ but are reproducible per seed.
3. Reset belief between runs and verify the initial state is canonical (categorical(1/6) over fd_class).

Belief updates:
4. Initialize belief at uniform. Observe a successful regular-file fread. Belief over fd_class should shift toward RegularFile (Bayesian update).
5. Observe an EAGAIN. Belief should shift toward Pipe or Socket.
6. Observe an EINTR. Belief should shift slightly toward Tty or Socket.
7. After 100 successful regular-file reads, belief.RegularFile should be > 0.95.

Action correctness:
8. Belief = high RegularFile, observation = short read. Action should be NormalReturn (regular files often have short reads at EOF).
9. Belief = high Pipe, observation = short read. Action should be RetryUntilSatisfied (pipe short reads are usually transient).
10. Belief = high Socket, observation = EAGAIN. Action should be ReturnShortAndSetErr (caller should select() then retry).
11. Belief = high Tty, observation = EINTR. Action should be RetryOnce.

Strict-mode bypass:
12. Same call sequence under strict mode emits zero POMDP records (the bypass is total).

Golden snapshot:
13. A canonical workload (read /etc/hosts byte by byte, write to /tmp/copy, fseek back, fread blocks) produces a stable action sequence captured in tests/runtime_math/golden/stdio_decisions.v1.json. The test re-runs and asserts byte-equal.

## Done

- All 13 tests pass deterministically across 100 consecutive runs
- Golden snapshot file checked in
- The snapshot is updated only via a [pomdp-snapshot-bump] PR label","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:40:57.572838231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:40:57.572838231Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["golden","pomdp","stdio","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.49","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:40:57.572838231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.49","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:40:57.572838231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.5","title":"stdio: rewrite fopen/fopen64/fdopen to be native-only, returning *mut NativeFile","description":"## Background

The audit pinpoints these as the only unconditional host delegators in stdio_abi.rs. fopen at L676-729 calls host_fopen() under prefer_host_stdio_streams() and otherwise falls back to native openat + registry insert. fdopen at L4060-4108 same pattern. Every other stdio function downstream "checks registry first; delegates to host if alien" — meaning the *only* way alien pointers enter the system is through these two constructors. Eliminate the alien-pointer source and the rest of the cleanup follows naturally.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (NativeFile must be _IO_FILE_plus-shaped before fopen/fdopen can return it without breaking existing pointer-arith assumptions).

## What to do

1. In crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdio_abi.rs, rewrite fopen() (L676-729):
   - Parse mode string into open flags (existing helper).
   - Call native openat raw syscall (already at L703-709 in the fallback branch — promote to primary).
   - Allocate a NativeFile from the slab allocator (or via the registry constructor — verify which exists).
   - Initialize: fd, vtable=&NATIVE_IO_JUMP_T, generation=fresh, mbstate=initial, orientation=Undecided, lock_count=0, open_flags=oflags, fingerprint=fresh siphash.
   - Set buffering mode based on isatty() raw syscall (NOT libc::isatty): _IOLBF if isatty, _IOFBF otherwise.
   - Insert into stream registry.
   - Return as *mut FILE (cast). NEVER call host_fopen.
2. fopen64 is an alias to fopen on 64-bit platforms — verify it stays that way and routes to the native path.
3. fdopen at L4060-4108 same surgery: take the input fd, build a NativeFile shell, set buffering mode based on isatty(fd), insert into registry, return.
4. Remove the prefer_host_stdio_streams() check from both functions. The function itself stays for now (deleted in a separate cleanup bead) but these two callers must not consult it.
5. Confirm the existing native-fallback branch in fopen (L694-729) becomes the primary and only branch.

## Cross-cutting

- The slab allocator for NativeFile should be a fixed-size pool to keep alloc/free deterministic. Coordinate with the malloc crate maintainer.
- isatty must be implemented as a raw ioctl(TCGETS) or fcntl probe — using libc::isatty would re-introduce host delegation.

## Done

- fopen/fopen64/fdopen do not call host_*_fn() under any branch
- A unit test calls fopen("/dev/null", "w"), inspects the returned pointer, confirms its in the registry, and verifies the vtable pointer is set
- Existing fopen tests still pass
- The fopen body has zero references to prefer_host_stdio_streams or host_fopen_fn","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:27:26.044561856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:21:10.749062074Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["constructor","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.5","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:27:26.044561856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.5","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:27:26.044561856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.5","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.50","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:21:10.748094523Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-9chy.50","title":"stdio: initialize _IO_FILE._lock pointer and maintain _IO_FILE._chain linked list in NativeFile","description":"## Background (refinement round addendum to bd-9chy.1)

Two critical _IO_FILE layout fields were not addressed by bd-9chy.1:

### _lock pointer (crash risk: HIGH)

In glibc _IO_FILE, the _lock field (offset ~136 on x86_64) is a _IO_lock_t* — a pointer to a recursive lock. Any code that calls flockfile() via the glibc layout dereferences this pointer. If we zero-fill the _IO_FILE header, _lock is NULL, and flockfile dereferences NULL → SIGSEGV.

Our NativeFileState already contains a parking_lot::ReentrantMutex (from bd-9chy.17). The _lock field in the _IO_FILE header region must point to this mutex.

### _chain linked list (functional risk: MEDIUM)

glibc maintains a global _IO_list_all pointer (head of a singly-linked list of all FILE*). Each _IO_FILE._chain field points to the next FILE in the list. Programs calling fcloseall(), and exit() flush-all (the atexit handler), walk this chain. If our NativeFiles are not linked, fcloseall crashes or silently misses files.

## What to do

### _lock pointer

1. When constructing any NativeFile, set `self._io_file._lock = &self._frankenlibc_state.recursive_mutex as *const _ as *mut _`.
2. This must happen in EVERY constructor: fopen, fdopen, freopen, popen, tmpfile, fmemopen, open_memstream, open_wmemstream, fopencookie, adopt_foreign_file.
3. The pointer must remain valid for the lifetime of the NativeFile (it always is, since the mutex is embedded in the struct).
4. Static assert: offset_of!(NativeFile, _frankenlibc_state.recursive_mutex) matches the location that _io_file._lock points to (verifiable at compile time via memoffset).
5. Test: a C program that calls flockfile(fp) / funlockfile(fp) via the _IO_FILE layout (not through FrankenLibCs ABI) must not crash. This simulates the worst case: a binary that bypasses our flockfile and goes directly to _lock.

### _chain linked list

1. Declare a global `static _IO_LIST_ALL: Mutex<*mut NativeFile>` in io_internal_abi.rs.
2. On every NativeFile construction, prepend to the list: `self._io_file._chain = *_IO_LIST_ALL.lock(); *_IO_LIST_ALL.lock() = self as *mut NativeFile;`.
3. On every NativeFile close (fclose), walk the list and unlink.
4. Export the `_IO_list_all` symbol in libc.map under GLIBC_2.2.5 so binaries that resolve it directly get our list.
5. The __cxa_atexit flush walker (bd-9chy.30) should walk _IO_list_all instead of a separate registry, unifying the two mechanisms.
6. Test: call fcloseall() after opening 5 files → all 5 are closed and their data flushed.
7. Test: open 3 files, walk _IO_list_all via a C program that follows _chain pointers → finds all 3.

### Why this is its own bead instead of being part of bd-9chy.1

bd-9chy.1 defines the struct layout. This bead wires the two dynamic fields that the layout alone does not cover (_lock pointer + _chain list maintenance). They require runtime logic in every constructor and destructor, which is more than a layout definition.

## Done

- _lock pointer initialized in all constructors; static_assert on offset
- _chain list maintained; _IO_list_all exported
- Direct flockfile-via-layout test passes
- fcloseall test passes
- _chain walk test passes
- exit flush walker (bd-9chy.30) uses _IO_list_all","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:19:42.474275446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:19:42.474275446Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["_io_file","foundation","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.50","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:19:42.474275446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.50","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:19:42.474275446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

### _lock pointer (crash risk: HIGH)

In glibc _IO_FILE, the _lock field (offset ~136 on x86_64) is a _IO_lock_t* — a pointer to a recursive lock. Any code that calls flockfile() via the glibc layout dereferences this pointer. If we zero-fill the _IO_FILE header, _lock is NULL, and flockfile dereferences NULL → SIGSEGV.

Our NativeFileState already contains a parking_lot::ReentrantMutex (from bd-9chy.17). The _lock field in the _IO_FILE header region must point to this mutex.

### _chain linked list (functional risk: MEDIUM)

glibc maintains a global _IO_list_all pointer (head of a singly-linked list of all FILE*). Each _IO_FILE._chain field points to the next FILE in the list. Programs calling fcloseall(), and exit() flush-all (the atexit handler), walk this chain. If our NativeFiles are not linked, fcloseall crashes or silently misses files.

## What to do

### _lock pointer

1. When constructing any NativeFile, set `self._io_file._lock = &self._frankenlibc_state.recursive_mutex as *const _ as *mut _`.
2. This must happen in EVERY constructor: fopen, fdopen, freopen, popen, tmpfile, fmemopen, open_memstream, open_wmemstream, fopencookie, adopt_foreign_file.
3. The pointer must remain valid for the lifetime of the NativeFile (it always is, since the mutex is embedded in the struct).
4. Static assert: offset_of!(NativeFile, _frankenlibc_state.recursive_mutex) matches the location that _io_file._lock points to (verifiable at compile time via memoffset).
5. Test: a C program that calls flockfile(fp) / funlockfile(fp) via the _IO_FILE layout (not through FrankenLibCs ABI) must not crash. This simulates the worst case: a binary that bypasses our flockfile and goes directly to _lock.

### _chain linked list

1. Declare a global `static _IO_LIST_ALL: Mutex<*mut NativeFile>` in io_internal_abi.rs.
2. On every NativeFile construction, prepend to the list: `self._io_file._chain = *_IO_LIST_ALL.lock(); *_IO_LIST_ALL.lock() = self as *mut NativeFile;`.
3. On every NativeFile close (fclose), walk the list and unlink.
4. Export the `_IO_list_all` symbol in libc.map under GLIBC_2.2.5 so binaries that resolve it directly get our list.
5. The __cxa_atexit flush walker (bd-9chy.30) should walk _IO_list_all instead of a separate registry, unifying the two mechanisms.
6. Test: call fcloseall() after opening 5 files → all 5 are closed and their data flushed.
7. Test: open 3 files, walk _IO_list_all via a C program that follows _chain pointers → finds all 3.

### Why this is its own bead instead of being part of bd-9chy.1

bd-9chy.1 defines the struct layout. This bead wires the two dynamic fields that the layout alone does not cover (_lock pointer + _chain list maintenance). They require runtime logic in every constructor and destructor, which is more than a layout definition.

## Done

- _lock pointer initialized in all constructors; static_assert on offset
- _chain list maintained; _IO_list_all exported
- Direct flockfile-via-layout test passes
- fcloseall test passes
- _chain walk test passes
- exit flush walker (bd-9chy.30) uses _IO_list_all","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:19:42.474275446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:40:07.502308889Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:40:07.502041623Z","close_reason":"Implemented _lock pointer initialization and _chain linked list. _IO_list_all is maintained on register/unregister. Chain tests pass: chain_stdio_streams_are_linked, chain_new_file_becomes_head, chain_unregister_unlinks_from_list, chain_walk_visits_all_streams, chain_io_file_layout_lock_ptr_is_valid. fcloseall/exit walker deferred to bd-9chy.30.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["_io_file","foundation","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.50","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:19:42.474275446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.50","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:19:42.474275446Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.51","title":"stdio: fflush(NULL) must walk _IO_list_all (not just the registry) after _chain infrastructure lands","description":"## Background (refinement round addendum)

POSIX requires fflush(NULL) to flush ALL open output streams. Currently in stdio_abi.rs, fflush(NULL) walks the FrankenLibC stream registry. After bd-9chy.50 introduces the _IO_list_all linked list (the glibc-compatible _chain mechanism), fflush(NULL) should walk _IO_list_all instead — this is the canonical mechanism that third-party code expects.

Additionally, the fclose path in bd-9chy.50 has a subtle lock-ordering constraint: the _IO_list_all lock must NOT be held while flushing the buffer (which may do I/O and take per-FILE locks). The correct order is:

1. Acquire per-FILE lock (flockfile semantics)
2. Flush the buffer via native write
3. Release per-FILE lock
4. Acquire _IO_list_all lock
5. Unlink from the chain
6. Release _IO_list_all lock
7. Free the NativeFile if non-standard stream

Reversing steps 2 and 4 causes deadlock if another thread is simultaneously walking _IO_list_all for fflush(NULL).

## What to do

1. Modify fflush(NULL) in stdio_abi.rs to walk _IO_list_all instead of the registry.
2. For each FILE in the chain: acquire per-FILE lock, flush if dirty and opened for writing, release lock.
3. Modify fclose to use the 7-step ordering above.
4. Test: open 5 files, write to each but dont fclose. Call fflush(NULL). Verify all 5 files have data on disk (stat the files).
5. Test: two threads running fflush(NULL) concurrently with a third thread running fclose on one of the files — no deadlock, no crash.
6. Test: fflush(NULL) after fclose on some files — the closed files are not in the chain and are not flushed (not an error).

## Done

- fflush(NULL) walks _IO_list_all
- Lock ordering documented and tested
- 3 tests pass including the concurrent stress test","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:21:36.628058885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:21:36.628058885Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flush-semantics","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.51","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:21:36.628058885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.51","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:21:36.628058885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.51","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.50","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:21:36.628058885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.6","title":"stdio: implement popen/pclose natively via posix_spawn + native fdopen","description":"## Background

popen is FILE-acquisition channel #3 in plan v4 §1.10. Today it lives in process_abi.rs or stdio_abi.rs (verify) and almost certainly delegates to host libc since host pipe+fork+fdopen is non-trivial to replicate. After the fopen/fdopen bead lands (bd-9chy.5), popen must build a native NativeFile around the pipe end.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1 (layout), bd-9chy.5 (native fdopen exists)

## What to do

1. Locate the current popen implementation. Likely in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/process_abi.rs.
2. Replace with: native pipe2 raw syscall, native posix_spawn raw syscall (or fork+execve fallback for kernels without posix_spawn), close the unused pipe end in the parent, pass mode "r" or "w" to native fdopen on the surviving end.
3. pclose: read fd from the NativeFile, close it via native fclose path, then waitpid on the recorded child pid, return the child status. Store the child pid in a NativeFile extension field (add a popen_child_pid: Option<i32> to NativeFileState).
4. Both "r" and "w" modes must work; "re" (close-on-exec) must be honored.
5. An integration test runs `popen("echo hello", "r")`, fgets a line, pclose, asserts return == 0 and the line == "hello\n".

## Edge cases

- SIGCHLD handling: do not install a default SIG_IGN that would orphan zombies; let the user decide.
- popen inside a multi-threaded program: pthread_atfork handlers (separate bead bd-9chy-22 in the locking tier) must run before fork.
- Failure modes: spawn failure must close both pipe ends and return NULL with errno set.

## Done

- popen/pclose are native; no host delegation
- Both modes work; "re" honored
- Integration test passes
- popen_child_pid field added to NativeFileState","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:27:38.977289670Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:21:10.877343411Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["constructor","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.6","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:27:38.977289670Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.6","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:27:38.977289670Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.6","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.50","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:21:10.876153609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-9chy.7","title":"stdio: implement tmpfile/tmpfile64 natively via O_TMPFILE + native fdopen","description":"## Background

Channel #4 in plan v4 §1.10. POSIX tmpfile creates an unnamed temporary file that is auto-deleted on close. Linux >= 3.11 provides O_TMPFILE for atomic creation of an unnamed file in a directory; older kernels need mkstemp + unlink.

## Depends on

bd-9chy.1, bd-9chy.5

## What to do

1. Locate current tmpfile in stdio_abi.rs (verify).
2. Implement: try openat(AT_FDCWD, "/tmp", O_TMPFILE|O_RDWR, 0600). If succeeded, build a NativeFile around the fd via native fdopen path.
3. Fallback for older kernels: use a fresh mkstemp template (e.g. /tmp/franken-tmp-XXXXXX), open O_RDWR|O_CREAT|O_EXCL, then immediately unlink the path while keeping the fd open. Build NativeFile around it.
4. tmpfile64 is the alias on 64-bit platforms; verify it routes to the same code.
5. Test: tmpfile, fwrite "hello", fseek SEEK_SET, fread back, assert "hello", fclose. Verify the file is gone after fclose by trying to readdir /tmp for any leftover.

## Done

- O_TMPFILE primary path; mkstemp+unlink fallback
- No libc::tmpfile or host_*_fn references
- Integration test passes including the no-leftover check","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:27:46.987767309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:21:10.998078663Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["constructor","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.7","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:27:46.987767309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.7","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:27:46.987767309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9chy.7","depends_on_id":"bd-9chy.50","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:21:10.996984802Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
